text: The citric acid cycle oxidizes acetyl-CoA in the mitochondrial matrix producing NADH and FADH2
Glycolysis converts glucose to pyruvate generating ATP in the cytoplasm of all cells
Nucleotide biosynthesis requires ribose-5-phosphate and involves multiple enzymatic steps
Oxidative phosphorylation couples electron transport to ATP synthesis in mitochondria
The pentose phosphate pathway generates NADPH for biosynthesis and antioxidant defense
Amino acid metabolism includes transamination and deamination reactions in liver mitochondria
Fatty acid synthesis is catalyzed by acetyl-CoA carboxylase and fatty acid synthase
Gluconeogenesis synthesizes glucose from non-carbohydrate substrates in fasting states
